Yesterday:A trip up to the Indiana Dunes State Park, which included a 90minute traffic jam for the last mile into the park 😅, to meet up with friends & dip ourselves in Lake Michigan! So much fun that we forgot to take pictures! But we got these! .bsky.social .bsky.social
Hit up Saugatuck Dunes State Park for the first time in a few years. The lake was cold. The hike was beautiful. The goddamn dogs were unleashed. What a place.

today's #naturespam is from the north shore of lake superior near the now defunct obatanga provincial park (which you can still hop around the gate & walk around). if you go, please be bear aware. i didn't see any but there was a lot of evidence of bears.

In the late 1800s, early glampers would set up a tent camp with loungers, hammocks and furnishings at the foot of Mt. Baldhead in Saugatuck. Their famous stairs and tower overlooking Lake Michigan were built in 1884 and created Saugatuck’s legacy as a beach town.
www.mlive.com/life/2025/09...

Spend time scoping out the restaurant scene in Bloomington. Range of ethnicities and food quality is top notch. IN Dunes are worth a stop. Known as the birthplace of ecology, they serve as both a historical point and cool reflection of Great Lakes geology. Mass Avenue is the hot spot in Indy.
In the Lake Michigan dunes, ten years apart.

Oval Beach dunes on Lake Michigan in Saugatuck Michigan

Saugatuck Dunes hike today. Still some ice in the lake and scattered on the beach. Also many more dead fish and birds than normal. (No pictures of those.) #dunes #winterphotography
It is. Especially in the summer. There's a state park nearby where you can walk the giant sand dunes and see Lake Michigan below.
I also did Grand Mere State Park up the Lake Michigan coast. It also has sand dunes but I spent most of my time in the woods and near the marsh.
If you see this quote post with a beach photo from your gallery Great Lakes make great beaches! Warren Dunes, Michigan
Visit our 4 great lakes if you can. But definitely the lake michigan sand dunes. If you cant make it north, Warren dunes state park is 2 1/2 hours down i-94.

I realize I should have threaded these but if anyone sees this and is curious - this is Indiana Dunes State Park near Chesterton and Valparaiso (where we usually stay) at the literal bottom of Lake Michigan and which sits next to the National Park.

It’s incredible how much this resembles Lake Michigan (inland sea) on the west coast of Michigan on a windy day.
If you’re in the states and homesick, go to Grand Mere State Park, Sleeping Bear dunes, or Petoskey State Park near Traverse City.
Dunes? I live in the dunes along Lake Michigan and that photo would be after a windy day out here 😂

Real-time coastlines, real-world insights. Check out the LIVE feed of Lake Michigan at Indiana Dunes State Park Beach – supporting recreationalists, researchers, and the broader public, linked here: www.webcoos.org/cameras/indu...

A geographical curiosity: Wolf & Savanna Lakes located in Minnesota's Savanna Portage State Park are less than a 1/2 mile apart yet lay on opposite sides of a hydrological divide. Wolf Lake drains to Lake Superior and the Atlantic Ocean. Savanna out the Mississippi River and into the Gulf of Mexico.

Due to deforestation, the dunes at Sandbanks Provincial Park were allowed to drift inland from Lake Ontario all the way to West Lake, destroying a road and a town in the process. This was in the late 1800s.

Just hike Warren Dunes State Park along Lake Michigan. Beautiful! The first Spring flowers are out - jack-in-the-pulpit, maypole, trillium. And, the plastic is out too. Picked up some. Over 90% of water samples from the Great Lakes contain microplastics.
One of the draws of the Great Lakes is their windswept, dune-filled shores. And Van Buren State Park outside of South Haven is known for just that.

Fayette State Park, Snail Shell Harbor, Big Bay de Noc, Lake Michigan. Fayette is a charcoal pig-iron ghost town with many of the original structures still standing, preserved as a state park in the 50's. Clear day, must stop, because who knows next time around? Favorite place in MI. A must-see.

Scenes from 2023: View from the limestone cliff trails at Fayette Historic State Park, Snail Shell Harbor, Big Bay de Noc, Lake Michigan. Great views of the ghost town and harbor from this trail at various clearings. Be sure and walk it if you’re ever there.
The Lake Michigan shore at Warren Dunes. Summer of 2011

I rode my bike out to Ottawa Sands Park today. The county's major improvements to the park are spectacular. At the center is a groundwater fed pond, remnant of the area's former life as a sand mine. New yurts, "treehouse" cabins, short walk to North Ottawa Dunes & North Shore Beach on Lake Michigan.

Two views of Lake Michigan at Warren Dunes State Park yesterday. cloudy after rain—
watchers at the placid shore view Lake Michigan
